programmes.show_description: Gloves Off: The popular mystery series starring James Bolam and Amanda Redman. The gun used to kill a boxer years ago surfaces in an armed robbery. Maggie O'Neill guest stars.
My Brother's Keeper: Sister Reginald's ex-con brother Alfie shows up at the convent. The sisters agree to let him stay, but when an artist is found murdered, all eyes are on Alfie.
Trust: Tracey falls for her smooth therapist Gerald and argues with Darryl about the lack of trust in their relationship when he bans her from continuing her sessions.
Smoke and Mirrors: First Chapter: Mattie searches for new clues about her missing mother, but an onstage murder in a production of Jane's book brings her to the theatre.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Up in Smoke: Part 2: The relief have to cover for Jim Carver yet again when he arrives at the station still very drunk.
Homework: Ackland's romantic evening away from Sun Hill spirals into something more sinister when a neighbour appears on her doorstep - carrying a shotgun.
Knowing You: Holmes falls out with Proctor when he publicly undermines her.
Consumers: Jim Carver has gone missing, and fears for his safety grow when a youth is arrested in possession of his credit card.
Lock In: Jim Carver hits rock bottom when Burnside is forced to arrest his old friend for murder.
A distant cousin of Fred's who has Down's syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his mother. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House.
Valerie treats a pregnant Somali woman who has undergone female genital circumcision, but who goes into hiding when the Nonnatus Team arrange for her to go into hospital.
Geordie heartthrob Robson Green stars in this feature-length 19th century drama. A rent collector in South Shields uses his talents as a card player to improve his lot.
Everyone's favourite Napoleonic hero embarks on another perilous mission. Sharpe arrives in India and learns that a British general's daughter has been kidnapped.
Lights, Camera, Murder!: A TV crew arrives to use St Vincent's Convent as a location for a spy thriller; the filming becomes all too real when a live gun is fired instead of a replica.
Flight: Emilia Fox stars in a feature-length forensics mystery. The murder of a well-known anti-fundamentalist Muslim, Amir Aziz, leads the team to a local taxi driver.
Ice Cream Wars: Dennis Waterman teams up with James Bolam in the hit cop drama. The case of the ice cream bandit, an armed robber who targeted ice cream vans, is re-opened.
A distant cousin of Fred's who has Down's syndrome is taken in by the Buckles following the death of his mother. New recruit Valerie prepares for her first day at Nonnatus House.
Legacy: Miep fights to keep Otto's business running in the hope that the Franks will return home. After the Allies liberate Amsterdam, Miep searches for Jewish residents of The Annex.
Warped: Mick and Shazia visit Cornwall's famous Towan Studios where some rare rock memorabilia has been stolen and the owner killed.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Poetic Justice: Comedy with the Chigwell sisters and their man-mad neighbour. On a prison visit to the Isle of Wight, Dorien finally meets Chris.
Money For Nothing: When the Sun Hill team form a syndicate to buy a greyhound, Quinnan's Jenny discovers she is part of it.
New. Making a Splash: Eva and Ronnie find common ground discussing a recent criminal case during a radio debate on modern policing, and the pair confide in one other off-air.
New. Photo Finish: A dead body is found in the new campus surgery just as Julia is making the finishing touches to the grand opening.
In at the Deep End: Connie Beauchamp joins the team and immediately gets on the wrong side of Will. Ric spends the day flirting with the new arrival before she reminds him who's in charge.
Hands On: Jordan takes drastic measures as the department is pushed to breaking point by a major incident and it becomes apparent it is likely to fail its inspection.
Money For Nothing: When the Sun Hill team form a syndicate to buy a greyhound, Quinnan's Jenny discovers she is part of it.
New. It's Mitchell v Mitchell at the beauty salon auction, but will the loser find a better way to get even? Phil tries to woo Shirley with mixed results.
New. Shirley uncovers Phil's big surprise and is filled with utter dread, and Ronnie has a huge decision to make. Ian begins to home school Lucy - can he cut it as a teacher?
When Dreams Come True: Fun and drama with the Yorkshire vets patrolling the livestock in the 1930s. Helen is finally up and about, but chaos is just around the corner.
Leo and Abby get ready for the Round the Island yacht race, while Polly reveals she intends to leave Gerald.
Teach Me, Dear Creature: Frank and Lu delve into the competitive world of private tutoring, while a reunion presents Sebastian with a choice about his future.
Keep Off the Grass: The classic Essex girls sitcom. Tracey and Sharon are faced with a moral dilemma when they witness the getaway of a criminal they know after a hold-up.
Some Adventures of the Inventor of the Mother Stitch: Young-at-heart sitcom, here in its 30th series! Hobbo discovers that the newly divorced Morton is lonely and depressed.
The Mother of All Mistakes - Or Is It?: Roy Clarke's long-running sitcom, still with a spring in its step! Adopted as a baby, Hobbo is determined to trace his natural mother.
Will Howard Cross the Atlantic Single-Handed?: More senior silliness in the record-breaking sitcom. Hobbo is having trouble getting Nelly to admit that she's his natural birth mother.
Silent Killer: A gripping, controversial case for Martin Shaw as the heroic High Court Judge. Deed presides over a case brought by a victim of motor neurone disease.
Ice Cream Wars: Dennis Waterman teams up with James Bolam in the hit cop drama. The case of the ice cream bandit, an armed robber who targeted ice cream vans, is re-opened.
In Pursuit of the Proper Sinner: A feature-length slice of mystery from top writer Elizabeth George. A double murder on the moors takes Lynley and Havers to the Peak District.
